title: What Did Yui See In Gendo
display_title: What Did Yui See In Gendo
wikitext:
[]: What Did Yui See In Gendo?             Quote Post by Ray » Fri Apr 18, 2014 5:31 pm It's obvious she loved him enough to marry him. . . but why? While not the bastard who tricked his own son yet, he wasn't exactly the most Moral of people either. The guy got in barfight's for heavens sake! Is there something I'm not seeing here?

[]:             Quote Post by sulla » Fri Apr 18, 2014 5:39 pm Morality has nothing to do with attraction it is completely amoral. Also he seemed to be a rebellious intellectual type to me. People like that tend to have an intensity that can be very attractive. Or maybe Yui just likes her bad boys? lolol :p

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Apr 18, 2014 5:58 pm There's a reason he put that horn on Eva-01's helmet.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Fri Apr 18, 2014 6:04 pm There's also the question of Yui's character: did she form a relationship with Gendo because he was the kind of person she could manipulate to help her achieve her own goals? That, of course, depends on whether you think of Yui as being a sort of villain or not (or being just as devious as Gendo). That being said, while see Gendo is an amoral guy, a lot of that is a front he builds for himself. When we are given glimpses into his mind, it's clear that the person Gendo is most like in the show...is Shinji. He hates himself, and deliberately keeps Shinji away because he thinks he is no good for Shinji. The irony of course, being, that by keeping himself distant like that, he does more damage to Shinji. Frankly, I think the Gendo that Yui 'has,' and the Gendo that we see, are two entirely different people. Even if you subscribe to the idea of Gendo as being an unrepentant villain, even the worst men alive have people they love, and who love them back.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Apr 18, 2014 6:15 pm Gob Hobblin wrote: Frankly, I think the Gendo that Yui 'has,' and the Gendo that we see, are two entirely different people.

[]:             Quote Post by Wanderlust » Fri Apr 18, 2014 6:19 pm Gob Hobblin wrote: Frankly, I think the Gendo that Yui 'has,' and the Gendo that we see, are two entirely different people.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Apr 18, 2014 6:51 pm Didn't she say something about Gendo's kinder or gentler side?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Apr 18, 2014 7:09 pm How does not everyone on this site keep the episode transcripts on their computer like I do? Episode 21 wrote: FUYUTSUKI (MONO): And back then, seasons and autumn still existed in this country. FUYUTSUKI: Are you serious? YUI: Yes, I am seeing Mr. Rokubungi. FUYUTSUKI (MONO): When I heard that, I wasn't able to hide my surprise. FUYUTSUKI: I never would have thought you would share your time with him. YUI: Oh, but Professor Fuyutsuki, he's quite a sweet person. It's just that no one knows it. FUYUTSUKI: Maybe it's more blissful not to know.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Fri Apr 18, 2014 10:16 pm NemZ wrote: Also, personal headcanon? I have this theory that Yui is the one that started that fight with some dude who was a little handsy, and Gendo (a complete stranger at the time) backed her up then took the fall while she escaped punishment.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Apr 19, 2014 12:06 am Monk Ed wrote: How does not everyone on this site keep the episode transcripts on their computer like I do?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Apr 19, 2014 12:31 am Rei IV wrote: You always have such interesting headcanon/theories for Gendo. Would such a scenario/headcanon be the moment Gendo gives his own leash to Yui.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sat Apr 19, 2014 12:45 am I am frankly surprised there isn't more speculation on the topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Apr 19, 2014 12:48 am I'm starting to think that Yui was the socially awkward daughter of a rich man and Gendo was the also socially awkward but kinda creepy Nice Guy that managed to badger her into marrying him and eventually she realized she'd borne this weirdo's kid and was like "fuck this shit I'm going to space". Afterwards he was a blank slate that the Akagis projected whatever they wanted onto. Naoko, mostly.Ritsuko just wanted to be her mom's eskimo brother.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Sat Apr 19, 2014 1:52 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: There's a reason he put that horn on Eva-01's helmet.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sat Apr 19, 2014 2:02 pm She saw someone who she will be able to easily manipulate and who under proper circumstances will be ruthless enough to help her in her messianistic delusions.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Apr 20, 2014 4:24 am Chuckman wrote: I'm starting to think that Yui was the socially awkward daughter of a rich man

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Apr 20, 2014 4:34 pm Monk Ed wrote: How does not everyone on this site keep the episode transcripts on their computer like I do? [snip]

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Wed May 07, 2014 2:17 am After watching the tv series and EoE I always thought that Yui had planned everything from the beginning. Then during Rebuild they pretty much flat out said it. Ive always believed that she even planned her 'love' to Gendo. In that he was an intelligent man whom she could control and that would carry out her plans even after her 'death' Im sure somewhere the comments she made about him being sweet and stuff wasn't a lie and she did 'care' about him on some level but in the end he was another pawn on her Eva chess board.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 07, 2014 7:06 am ^She just needed a pair of working testicles to provide another 23 chromosomes for her messiah, you mean? I think that's taking it to far. The only indications of her motives we get, which we can of course doubt, but get no real counterpoint to, is that she turned against SEELE because she had a kid. Meeting Gendo came first, then came the final incarnation of her plan. One thing she did keep from her SEELE days is the deterministic mindset ("It will all happen as it must"), that Gendo interestingly subscribed to somewhat less. Maybe being able to find something cute or loveable about a outwardly rather flawed dude made her see that the world as it is was worthwhile after all? You could see her relatives telling her to not even bother with procreation since the world is going to end before the kid turns 15, and maybe she didn't like that thought. Not even 'dark conspiracy priestesses' are immune to oxytocine? Then again, she seems to have wanted kids to begin with, and maybe she even intended them for conspiracy use (her manga counterpart does seem to, but they're all a tad more psycho in mangaland), but then she thought, "zomfg teh cute baby~~ Don't you worry sweety, mommy is going to get herself superpowers and pwn the bad, bad SEELE/angels/etc so you can live out your life in peace... Then, I will go to space to tell the rest of the universe about that time you pissed in my face while I was changing your diapers and eternally embarass you in front of the future civilizations of the milky way"

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed May 07, 2014 12:15 pm The notion of Gendo being a "Nice Guy" - as in a guy who is good to someone just to trade in goodness for favors in bed - just doesn't seem to be the case. Gendo had quite a bit of jerk/smartmouth to him in the beginning and I don't think he'd be able to act that way towards Yui. I have the impression that he was genuinely good to her in some way, rather than merely being "nice". If he were just interested in banging her, would he have suffered so much from her death? Stillborn wrote: She saw someone who she will be able to easily manipulate and who under proper circumstances will be ruthless enough to help her in her messianistic delusions.
